title: Celeste
edition_pinned: Celeste (Maddy Makes Games / Extremely OK Games, 2018 launch; Assist Mode as shipped) — Farewell DLC is optional post-content, not the pin
moat: One air dash plus climb stamina frames every screen as a limited-resource precision solve, not a health fight.

loop_skeleton.description: Enter a screen, spend the single air dash and climb stamina to chain geometry, die on contact with hazards, respawn at the screen entrance with full resources, repeat until the screen exits.

depth_mechanisms.name: dash budget
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Madeline has one air dash that recharges on solid ground (or specific midair pickups). Screens are authored so the dash is the scarce commit that gates routes; diagonal eight-way dash is part of the legal input space.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If players receive unlimited air dashes by default, average deaths-per-screen falls more than 50% and second-session retry time no longer tracks screen geometry difficulty.

depth_mechanisms.name: screen-scoped infinite retry
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Death restarts the current screen instantly with full dash/stamina; progress is room-exit, not a lives currency. The mountain map chains hundreds of screens so failure cost is seconds, not chapter restarts.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If death forces a multi-screen checkpoint rollback, completion rate for mid-game chapters drops and session length shortens because retry cost exceeds learning gain.

depth_mechanisms.name: assist mode opt-in break
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Assist Mode lets players change game speed, grant infinite dashes, invincibility, and other toggles without a separate story path. Designer states Assist breaks the fine-tuned difficulty by design so more players can finish.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If Assist Mode is removed and no equivalent toggles exist, a measurable subset of players who used Assist to finish never reach the ending (self-report / completion telemetry).

feel_signature: Input commits a dash or stamina slice within one screen; failure is instant full-resource retry at the screen door, so the decision is always the next frame sequence, not inventory or loadout.
second_session_answer: Players return to clear the next chapter screens or collect strawberries they skipped, using the same dash budget on harder geometry — not to spend meta currency.
packaging_lessons: Ship chapter 1 with dash+climb+instant retry before any collectible economy; GDC level-design talk centers on tools for hundreds of precision stages.
Expose Assist toggles as player-owned breaks of the design, not as a hidden difficulty-tier flag that strips story.
anti_lessons: Do not bolt an XP or loadout layer onto a dash-budget platformer — it hides which inputs failed.
Do not replace screen-scoped retry with long checkpoint gaps; the depth is high death count at low time cost.

system_bom.note: run-jump-dash-climb-retry is the only required combat-free loop
death is the primary feedback; no lives counter
screen and chapter progress persist; strawberries are optional persistence
dash ready state and stamina must be readable without opening menus
chapter unlocks and B-sides gate harder geometry using the same verbs

citations.claim: moat/dash budget: dash is central; climb contrasts as slower stamina-limited movement vs dash.
citations.source: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Imsdo3vwvPo (ACMI: Celeste conversation with Maddy Thorson & Noel Berry)

citations.claim: depth_mechanisms.screen design tools for 300+ hardcore stages and mountain map arrangement.
depth_mechanisms.assist mode opt-in break: Assist can alter speed, infinite dashes, invincibility; designer says Assist breaks the game and that is acceptable to empower players.
citations.source: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NInNVEHj_G4 (Game Maker's Toolkit: What Makes Celeste's Assist Mode Special)

citations.claim: edition/feel: Celeste released 2018-01-25; Assist Mode is part of the shipped accessibility design.
system_bom play-loop verbs and single-player platform structure.
